reasoning: Han Dong-hoon officially lost the People Power Party (PPP) presidential primary to Kim Moon-soo on May 3, 2025, securing only 43.47% of the vote. Following his defeat, Han conceded, stating "my journey ends here." Furthermore, when the PPP later rescinded Kim Moon-soo's nomination on May 10 due to internal conflicts, they replaced him with former Prime Minister Han Duck-soo, not Han Dong-hoon. Currently, Han is not a candidate in the election scheduled for June 3, 2025. Even if he were to mount a highly unlikely last-minute independent bid, his general polling was extremely low (5-10%) against the Democratic Party frontrunner Lee Jae-myung. Consequently, his chances of being elected the next president of South Korea are virtually zero. Setting to the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.
